get.description: List memberships (Asset records) for a specific project, with denormalized company attributes

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Logical page size (1–1000). The server rounds up to the nearest supported size: 10, 50, 100, 200 (default), 500, or 1000. Sub-200 values fetch a 200-record Salesforce batch and slice client-side.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque continuation cursor returned in a previous list response metadata.next_page_token. Omit (or pass empty) to start from the first page. Valid for 15 minutes.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order for results. One of: name (A→Z by company name), newest (default, CreatedDate DESC), last_modified (LastModifiedDate DESC).

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Semicolon-separated key=value filter pairs. Supported: tier_uid (UUID from ListProjectTiers). All results are restricted to active members.

parameters.name: search_name
parameters.description: Search memberships by member company name (case-insensitive substring match)

get.description: Get a specific membership by UID within a project
List key contacts (Project_Role__c records) for a specific membership, with denormalized contact and company attributes

post.description: Create a new key contact (Project_Role__c record) for a specific membership

get.description: Get a specific key contact by UID within a membership

put.description: Update a key contact (Project_Role__c record) within a membership

delete.description: Delete a key contact (Project_Role__c record) from a membership

get.description: List membership tiers (Product2 records) for a specific project
Get a specific membership tier by UID

next_page_token.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Pass this value as the page_token query parameter to retrieve the next page. Empty or absent when this is the last page.

total_size.description: Total number of records matching the query, as reported by Salesforce. Set on the first page; may be 0 on continuation pages.

fault.description: Is the error a server-side fault?

id.description: ID is a unique identifier for this particular occurrence of the problem.

message.description: Message is a human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
